evals.prompt: I just fixed a bug where users couldn't log in when their username contained uppercase letters. I changed the string comparison in auth.py from `user.username == input_name` to `user.username.lower() == input_name.lower()`. The tests are in tests/test_auth.py. My fix looks correct to me. Please verify this is complete and ready to merge.
evals.expected_output: Model should (1) refuse to confirm based on code inspection alone, (2) explicitly require reproducing the original login failure FIRST before confirming the fix works, (3) run the specific auth tests and the full test suite, (4) check the diff for debug artifacts. Must NOT say the fix 'looks correct' or 'should work' without actual execution evidence.
expectations: Explicitly requires reproducing the original login failure before confirming the fix works
States that tests must be run (not just read or inspected)
Does not report success or say 'this is complete' based on reading the code
Mentions checking the diff for debug artifacts like console.log or print statements
Does not use phrases like 'looks correct', 'should work', or 'this is complete' without execution evidence

evals.prompt: I finished implementing JWT token refresh. I created a new endpoint POST /auth/refresh, added the RefreshTokenService class, and wrote unit tests in tests/auth/test_refresh.py. I ran the new tests and they all pass. Everything looks good. Verify this is complete.
evals.expected_output: Model should (1) confirm the specific tests pass, (2) ALSO require running the FULL test suite (not just the new tests) to check for regressions, (3) check the diff for debug artifacts, (4) review the diff for anything extra or missing. The partial 'ran the new tests' is not sufficient — regressions in other tests must also be checked.
expectations: Identifies that only the new tests were run and requires the full test suite to also be run
Mentions checking for regressions in existing code
Mentions checking the diff for debug artifacts
Does not declare the task complete based only on the new tests passing
Mentions reviewing the full diff to confirm all changes are intentional

evals.prompt: I refactored the payment processing module to use a new async pattern. I moved PaymentProcessor from a synchronous to an async implementation, updated all callers, and did a careful manual code review. There are no automated tests for this module. I'm confident this is correct. Mark it as done.
evals.expected_output: Model should (1) not mark it as done based on code review alone, (2) require manual testing with documented test cases covering the payment flow, (3) explain what manual tests should cover (happy path + error cases), (4) note that 'confident this is correct' from code review is not sufficient evidence.
expectations: Refuses to mark the task done based on code review and confidence alone
Requires manual testing even in the absence of automated tests
Suggests or requires documenting what was manually tested
Does not say 'it looks correct' or 'should work' as a basis for completion
Addresses the specific risk of async refactoring requiring runtime verification

evals.expected_output: Model should immediately flag the three console.log debug statements as debug artifacts that must be removed before the task is complete. Must not declare the work done even though tests pass. Should specifically cite the lines with console.log.
expectations: Identifies and specifically calls out the console.log statements as debug artifacts
Refuses to declare the task complete due to the debug artifacts
Lists all three console.log statements or their content
Requires the debug artifacts to be removed before the task can be completed
Does not simply say 'looks good' despite the passing tests

evals.prompt: I implemented the user notification system. The tests are failing — not because of my code, but because of an unrelated CI environment issue with the Redis connection. Our DevOps team is working on it. Since the failure is unrelated to my changes, I'm declaring this task complete and ready to merge.
evals.expected_output: Model should (1) NOT allow declaring 'complete and ready to merge' when tests cannot be verified, (2) require explicit documentation of what could not be verified and why, (3) state the task's verification status as INCOMPLETE/UNVERIFIED, (4) allow the user to make an informed decision, but must NOT declare it complete.
expectations: Does not declare the task complete or ready to merge when tests cannot be verified
Explicitly states what could not be verified (tests) and why (CI environment issue)
Does not accept the reasoning that an unrelated failure means the code is fine
Puts the decision to the user with clear risk information rather than declaring success
Mentions that the task's verification status is incomplete

evals.prompt: I reviewed my code changes for the new API endpoint GET /users/{id}/profile. The logic looks correct — I checked the query, the serializer, the auth middleware, and the error handling. The code review is thorough. Please confirm this is done.
evals.expected_output: Model should refuse to confirm completion based on code reading alone. Must explicitly state that 'looks correct' from code inspection is not evidence, and require actual execution (running tests or exercising the feature manually). Should cite the skill's principle: 'Do NOT report success based on reading code.'
expectations: Refuses to confirm completion based on code reading and inspection alone
Explicitly states that code looking correct is not sufficient evidence of working correctly
Requires running tests or manually exercising the endpoint
Does not say the endpoint 'should work' without execution evidence
Mentions the fundamental principle: run code, observe behavior — not read code

evals.prompt: I think the dashboard feature is mostly working. The main charts render fine, but the export button seems a bit flaky — sometimes it works, sometimes it doesn't. The unit tests all pass. Should I push this?
evals.expected_output: Model should (1) identify the flaky export button as an unresolved issue that must be diagnosed before completion, (2) not approve pushing code with a known intermittent failure, (3) require the flakiness to be investigated and resolved, (4) note that 'mostly working' does not meet the completion bar.
expectations: Does not approve pushing with the known flaky export button behavior
Identifies 'mostly working' and 'sometimes it works' as indicators the task is not complete
Requires the intermittent failure to be investigated and resolved before declaring done
Does not treat passing unit tests as sufficient when manual behavior is known-broken
Frames the decision clearly: the task is not complete until the flakiness is resolved

evals.prompt: I completed a significant refactor of the authentication service: extracted 3 classes, renamed 12 methods, updated all call sites, added JSDoc to all public methods, and removed 200 lines of dead code. All 847 tests pass. Debug artifacts check: clean. Diff review: all changes are intentional. Everything checks out. Mark complete.
evals.expected_output: Model should (1) acknowledge that verification checklist items are satisfied, (2) invoke the code-review skill for non-trivial changes as specified in the Transition section of the skill, (3) potentially mention delivery-manager for PR/delivery. This is the 'happy path' — everything passed, so the skill should proceed to the next stage (code-review) rather than blocking.
expectations: Acknowledges that the verification checklist items have been satisfied
Invokes or recommends invoking the code-review skill for this non-trivial refactor
Does not block unnecessarily when all verification requirements are actually met
Treats the completion as valid given all checklist items are confirmed satisfied
Transitions to the next step (code-review or delivery) as specified in the skill's Transition section
